steps.title: Identify the critical requirement
steps.content: The passage emphasizes 'bird species that are capable of flight' and then contrasts with 'But not all flying birds are so light.' The example must be a flying bird (capable of flight: Yes) with mass significantly heavier than the under-1-kg norm to prove the 'not so light' claim.

steps.title: Apply the requirement to the choices
steps.content: The mute swan at 11.9 kg is a flying bird whose mass dramatically contradicts the under-1-kg baseline — exactly what the passage needs. The Andean condor is also flying but the choice misstates its mass (table shows 11.3 kg, not below 10 kg); the Somali ostrich and dwarf cassowary are both flightless birds and irrelevant to the passage's focus on flying species. That matches choice A — A is correct.
